title: Psychological first aid basics
summary: Practical guidance for “Psychological first aid basics” within “Community · Psychology · Records”. It supports recognition of danger signs, clean supportive care, accurate records, and timely escalation, produces a visible result, and states when to stop or move to a safer fallback.
content:
Practical guidance for “Psychological first aid basics” within “Community · Psychology · Records”. It supports recognition of danger signs, clean supportive care, accurate records, and timely escalation, produces a visible result, and states when to stop or move to a safer fallback.

Start with: Use a written trigger, one accountable owner, a visible result, and a scheduled review for this exact task. This is the opening check for “Psychological first aid basics” in “Community · Psychology · Records”.

Stop condition: Do not diagnose, prescribe, improvise dosing, perform invasive procedures, or delay emergency help. Apply this boundary to “Psychological first aid basics” in “Community · Psychology · Records”.

Reduced fallback: If “Psychological first aid basics” cannot be completed, do only the first verified step, mark the remaining capability unavailable, and use emergency services, a clinician, pharmacist, poison centre, or trained first-aid provider as appropriate.

steps: Use a written trigger, one accountable owner, a visible result, and a scheduled review for this exact task. This is the opening check for “Psychological first aid basics” in “Community · Psychology · Records”.

Make the scene safe, use consent where possible, and call emergency services for immediate threats. Record the result under “Community · Psychology · Records” before continuing “Psychological first aid basics”.

Observe breathing, alertness, major bleeding, severe pain, temperature exposure, and rapid change without inventing a diagnosis. Recheck this point before closing “Psychological first aid basics” for “Community · Psychology · Records”.

Give only low-risk care within training and follow the person’s established professional plan for medicines or chronic needs. Treat any uncertainty here as an unresolved item in “Psychological first aid basics” for “Community · Psychology · Records”.
Record time, observations, actions, medicines already taken, allergies, response, and who was contacted. A second person should be able to verify this part of “Psychological first aid basics”.

Reassess frequently and arrange qualified help early when danger signs, uncertainty, or deterioration are present. Include the outcome of “Psychological first aid basics” in the handover for “Community · Psychology · Records”.
warnings: Do not diagnose, prescribe, improvise dosing, perform invasive procedures, or delay emergency help. Apply this boundary to “Psychological first aid basics” in “Community · Psychology · Records”.
Protect privacy and infection-control boundaries, but never let paperwork delay lifesaving action. Apply this boundary to “Psychological first aid basics” in “Community · Psychology · Records”.

Apply this boundary to “Psychological first aid basics” in “Community · Psychology · Records”.

alternatives: If “Psychological first aid basics” cannot be completed, do only the first verified step, mark the remaining capability unavailable, and use emergency services, a clinician, pharmacist, poison centre, or trained first-aid provider as appropriate.
Use a dated paper note and spoken read-back for “Psychological first aid basics” in “Community · Psychology · Records” when the normal form, device, or communication channel is unavailable.
failure_signs: Stop and reassess “Psychological first aid basics” when breathing, alertness, circulation, pain, temperature, bleeding, or behaviour worsens. Apply this boundary to “Psychological first aid basics” in “Community · Psychology · Records”.

Stop and reassess “Psychological first aid basics” when the person cannot keep fluids down, develops severe allergic signs, chest symptoms, seizure, confusion, or another urgent red flag. Apply this boundary to “Psychological first aid basics” in “Community · Psychology · Records”.
when_not_to_use: Do not continue “Psychological first aid basics” while breathing, alertness, circulation, major bleeding, severe pain, seizure, poisoning, chest symptoms, pregnancy danger signs, or rapid deterioration is present. Apply this boundary to “Psychological first aid basics” in “Community · Psychology · Records”.

This card is not authorization to diagnose, prescribe, change a dose, perform an invasive procedure, force care, or delay 112 and qualified medical help. Topic-specific stop check for “Psychological first aid basics”: also follow this warning: “Do not diagnose, prescribe, improvise dosing, perform invasive procedures, or delay emergency help in “Psychological first aid basics”.”. Apply this boundary to “Psychological first aid basics” in “Community · Psychology · Records”.
short_term: Complete one verified cycle for “Psychological first aid basics” in “Community · Psychology · Records”, record its owner, time, result, unresolved risk, and next review.
long_term: Review “Psychological first aid basics” after real use in “Community · Psychology · Records”; replace failed materials, practise the handover, and update thresholds when official guidance or local conditions change.
if_method_fails: If the method described in “Psychological first aid basics” gives no verifiable result, stop relying on it, isolate the uncertain output, record where it failed, and switch to emergency services, a clinician, pharmacist, poison centre, or trained first-aid provider as appropriate.
environment_notes: Adapt the guidance in “Psychological first aid basics” for “Community · Psychology · Records” to weather, lighting, crowding, accessibility, power, water, transport, and available skill without removing the stop condition or independent check.
